repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: Follow up to: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40113 Adding `virt_lines_overflow` option to `virtual_lines` config. The default behavior remains the same as it was before, just allowing users more customization. Random LSP like message text to show multiple errors/warnings on a single line: Potential follow up: adding `virt_lines_overflow` option to codelens.

comment_data.body:
this is on a `VirtualLines` type already.

```suggestion
      • {overflow}?  (`'trunc'|'scroll'|'wrap'|'auto'`, default:
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -711,16 +711,19 @@ Lua module: vim.diagnostic                                    *diagnostic-api*
 *vim.diagnostic.Opts.VirtualLines*
 
     Fields: ~
-      • {current_line}?  (`boolean`, default: `false`) Only show diagnostics
-                         for the current line.
-      • {format}?        (`fun(diagnostic:vim.Diagnostic): string?`) A
-                         function that takes a diagnostic as input and returns
-                         a string or nil. If the return value is nil, the
-                         diagnostic is not displayed by the handler. Else the
-                         output text is used to display the diagnostic.
-      • {severity}?      (`vim.diagnostic.SeverityFilter`) Only show virtual
-                         lines for diagnostics matching the given severity
-                         |diagnostic-severity|
+      • {current_line}?         (`boolean`, default: `false`) Only show
+                                diagnostics for the current line.
+      • {format}?               (`fun(diagnostic:vim.Diagnostic): string?`) A
+                                function that takes a diagnostic as input and
+                                returns a string or nil. If the return value
+                                is nil, the diagnostic is not displayed by the
+                                handler. Else the output text is used to
+                                display the diagnostic.
+      • {severity}?             (`vim.diagnostic.SeverityFilter`) Only show
+                                virtual lines for diagnostics matching the
+                                given severity |diagnostic-severity|
+      • {virt_lines_overflow}?  (`'trunc'|'scroll'|'wrap'|'auto'`, default:

comment_data.body: This should not require a new test. Just add an assert to the `includes diagnostic code and message` test, above

       eq('miss-symbol: Missed symbol `,`', result[1][3][1])
     end)
 
+    it('uses virt_lines_overflow=auto by default', function()

comment_regular_data.body:
I think that only changing the currently hard coded `'scroll'` to hard coded `'auto'` is what most users would expect.

Adding new option here won't hurt much, but I'd also expect the default to be `'auto'`.

> I think that only changing the currently hard coded `'scroll'` to hard coded `'auto'` is what most users would expect.
> 
> Adding new option here won't hurt much, but I'd also expect the default to be `'auto'`.

I kept it 'scroll' to preserve the existing UX, but I think `auto` should be the default also. 

Updated it to be the default.

However note that `auto` has some performance overhead. Drawing a wrapped virtual line is O(n^2), although that can be optimized to O(n) by adding a few variables. Virtual lines that *may* wrap also have an O(n) counting overhead (compared to O(1) counting for `trunc` and `scroll`) on cursor movement or scrolling even if they don't actually wrap, and that isn't avoidable.

> Drawing a wrapped virtual line is O(n^2), although that can be optimized to O(n) by adding a few variables. Virtual lines that _may_ wrap also have an O(n) counting overhead (compared to O(1) counting for `trunc` and `scroll`)

Although slightly off-topic, could you please help clarifying what `n` here stands for? A virtual line width, window width, or something else?

> Although slightly off-topic, could you please help clarifying what `n` here stands for? 

Total number of bytes needed to store the virtual line.

title: feat(diagnostic): add virt_lines_overflow option for virtual_lines
